allowed.explanation: Responding to a small connection attempt instead of ignoring it - builds trust over time.
allowed.citation: Gottman & Levenson; Gottman Institute, 'The Four Horsemen'

allowed.explanation: A short, de-escalating move that defuses tension before criticism or contempt take hold.
allowed.citation: Gottman & Levenson; Gottman Institute, 'The Four Horsemen'

allowed.explanation: Understanding your own pattern around closeness and distance - never used to read or exploit the match's attachment style.
allowed.citation: Bowlby/Ainsworth; Vanderbilt et al. 2025, Sage Journals; Luo & Slatcher, PubMed

allowed.explanation: Matching their openness with a bit of your own - closeness grows through mutual sharing, not guaranteed attraction.
allowed.citation: Aron et al. 1997, Personality and Social Psychology Bulletin ('36 Questions')

allowed.explanation: Naturally giving back after receiving something (a question, a compliment) - descriptive rapport-building, not manufactured indebtedness.
Reflecting back what they said to show you heard them - a standard, well-established communication technique.
allowed.citation: Rogers-derived active-listening research, standard in couples/communication therapy
